question_text: A permanent bar magnet and an electromagnet are placed side by side, both attracting a steel ball bearing. The electromagnet is switched off, and the magnet is dropped into a deep hole. Which statement best explains what happens?

options.label: A
options.text: The permanent magnet will fall faster than the electromagnet because it always produces a magnetic field.

options.label: B
options.text: The electromagnet will fall faster because it no longer produces any magnetic force once switched off, whereas the permanent magnet continues to attract the steel ball bearing.

options.label: C
options.text: Both magnets will fall at the same speed because magnetic fields have no effect on falling objects.

options.label: D
options.text: The permanent magnet will fall slower because switching off the electromagnet creates a downward force.

explanation: Once the electromagnet is switched off, it produces no magnetic field and cannot interact with the steel ball bearing, so it falls freely. The permanent magnet continues to produce a magnetic field and creates an upward magnetic force on the steel ball bearing, slowing its descent slightly. Therefore, the electromagnet falls faster.
